questions.text: The process by which organisms take in food and utilize it for energy is called:
questions.explanation: Nutrition provides the necessary nutrients for growth and repair of the body.

questions.text: Autotrophic nutrition involves the synthesis of food from:
options: Inorganic sources like CO2 and water
questions.explanation: Green plants and some bacteria use photosynthesis to make food from simple inorganic molecules.

questions.text: Chlorophyll is primarily responsible for:
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll in chloroplasts traps solar energy to drive the process of photosynthesis.

questions.text: Tiny pores present on the surface of leaves for gas exchange are:
questions.explanation: Stomata facilitate the intake of CO2 and release of O2 and water vapor.

questions.text: Opening and closing of stomatal pores is controlled by:
questions.explanation: Guard cells swell when water flows into them, opening the pore.

questions.text: Which enzyme is found in human saliva?
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase breaks down starch into complex sugars like maltose.

questions.text: The inner lining of the stomach is protected from Hydrochloric acid by:
questions.explanation: Mucus forms a protective barrier to prevent the acid from eroding the stomach wall.

questions.text: The longest part of the alimentary canal is the:
questions.explanation: The small intestine is coiled and can be several meters long to provide surface for absorption.

questions.text: Bile juice is secreted by the ________ and stored in the ________.
questions.explanation: Bile helps in the emulsification of fats.

questions.text: Finger-like projections in the small intestine that increase surface area for absorption are:
questions.explanation: Villi are richly supplied with blood vessels to carry absorbed food to all cells.

questions.text: Breakdown of pyruvate using oxygen takes place in the:
questions.explanation: Aerobic respiration occurs in the mitochondria, releasing CO2, water, and energy.

questions.text: Accumulation of ________ in muscles causes cramps during sudden activity.
questions.explanation: Lactic acid is produced during anaerobic respiration in muscle cells when oxygen is insufficient.

questions.text: Energy currency of the cell is:
questions.explanation: Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) stores and provides energy for cellular activities.

questions.text: The balloon-like structures in lungs where gas exchange occurs are:
questions.explanation: Alveoli provide a large surface area for O2 to enter and CO2 to leave the blood.

questions.text: In humans, oxygen is carried by the respiratory pigment:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin in Red Blood Cells has a high affinity for oxygen.

questions.text: The human heart has ________ chambers.
questions.explanation: Four chambers prevent the mixing of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood.

questions.text: Which blood vessels have thick, elastic walls?
questions.explanation: Arteries carry blood away from the heart under high pressure.

questions.text: Platelets are responsible for:
questions.explanation: Platelets plug leaks in blood vessels by forming clots at injury sites.

questions.text: Xylem tissue in plants is responsible for the transport of:
questions.explanation: Xylem carries water and minerals upwards from roots to leaves.

questions.text: Transport of soluble products of photosynthesis is called:
questions.explanation: Translocation occurs through the phloem tissue.

questions.text: The structural and functional unit of the kidney is:
questions.explanation: Each kidney contains about a million nephrons that filter blood.

questions.text: The cup-shaped end of a nephron is called:
questions.explanation: It collects the filtrate from the glomerulus capillaries.

questions.text: Plants store their waste products in:
questions.explanation: Resins, gums, and falling leaves are methods of plant excretion.

questions.text: Artificial kidney works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Dialysis removes nitrogenous waste products from blood using a semi-permeable membrane.

questions.text: Which organ secretes Trypsin and Lipase?
questions.explanation: The pancreas secretes pancreatic juice containing enzymes for protein and fat digestion.

questions.text: Loss of water in the form of vapor from aerial parts of plants is:
questions.explanation: Transpiration creates a suction pull that draws water up the xylem.

questions.text: The rhythmic contraction and expansion of the walls of the alimentary canal is:
questions.explanation: Peristaltic movements push the food forward along the gut.

questions.text: Double circulation means:
options: Blood flows through the heart once
Blood flows through the heart twice in one complete cycle
Blood flows in two directions in one vessel
questions.explanation: In humans, blood passes through the heart twice (Pulmonary and Systemic circulation).

questions.text: Normal blood pressure in a healthy adult is:
questions.explanation: 120 represents systolic and 80 represents diastolic pressure.

questions.text: The pigment that gives blood its red color is:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin is an iron-containing protein that binds with oxygen.

questions.text: Photosynthesis occurs in which organelle?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll required for photosynthesis.

questions.text: Which component of blood is involved in fighting germs?
questions.explanation: White Blood Cells are the defense system of our body.

questions.text: Urea is produced in the ________ and removed by the ________.
questions.explanation: The liver converts toxic ammonia to urea, which the kidneys filter out.

questions.text: Energy released during respiration is immediately used to synthesize:
questions.explanation: ATP acts as the immediate source of energy for all cell processes.

questions.text: The first step of glucose breakdown (glycolysis) occurs in:
questions.explanation: One molecule of glucose is broken into two molecules of pyruvate in the cytoplasm.

questions.text: Which chamber of the heart receives oxygenated blood from the lungs?
questions.explanation: Oxygenated blood returns from the lungs via the pulmonary vein into the left atrium.

questions.text: Vessels that carry blood from the heart to all parts of the body are:
questions.explanation: Arteries (except pulmonary) carry oxygenated blood.

questions.text: The liquid part of the blood is called:
questions.explanation: Plasma contains proteins, nutrients, and waste products.

questions.text: Anaerobic respiration in yeast produces:
questions.explanation: This process is used in the brewing and baking industries.

questions.text: The vein that carries oxygenated blood is the:
questions.explanation: This is an exception; all other veins carry deoxygenated blood.
